findings.quote: The Company hereby appoints and grants Distributor the exclusive right to sell the products of the Company, including the Snotarator™ Nasal Aspirator,  ("Products") listed in the current "Price List" (Exhibit "A" attached hereto). The distribution right shall be limited to all countries and all of their territories and possessions within the continent known as South America ("Territory")  (Exhibit "B" attached hereto).
findings.why: Grants Distributor an exclusive right to sell Products within the defined South American territory, restricting Company from appointing other distributors there.

findings.quote: Distributor agrees that the Products, including the name Snotarator ™ Nasal Aspirator (the "Brand") are the sole property of the Company and Distributor has no interest whatsoever in such Brand and Products, and Distributor shall use the Brand and Products only for so long as the rights granted hereby remains in full force and effect.  Distributor shall not take any actions, or aid or assist any other party to take any actions that would infringe upon, harm or contest the proprietary rights of Company in and to the Brand and Products.
findings.why: Affirms Company's sole ownership of the Brand and Products and bars Distributor from challenging or contesting those IP rights.

findings.quote: Distributor shall not assign the rights granted in this Section 1.01 without the prior written consent of the Company.
findings.why: Prohibits Distributor from assigning its distribution rights without Company's prior written consent.

findings.quote: Distributor agrees not to represent or sell other products which are deemed to be competitive with the Company's Products unless agreed to by the Company by written notice.
findings.why: Restricts Distributor from representing or selling competing products without Company's written agreement.

findings.quote: Nor shall the Company at any time be liable for any incidental, special or consequential damages.
findings.why: Excludes incidental, special, and consequential damages from Company's liability — while framed as a limitation, it is an absolute carve-out of certain damage categories with no cap structure, exposing Distributor to bear those losses entirely.

findings.quote: Company agrees, at its own expense, to indemnify, defend and hold harmless each Distributor and its customers from and against every expense, damage, cost and loss (including attorneys' fees incurred) and to satisfy all judgments and decrees resulting from a claim, suit or proceeding insofar as it is based upon an allegation that any Product or any part thereof furnished by Company or any process which is practiced in the customary use of the Product is or has been infringing upon any patent, copyright or proprietary right, if Company is notified promptly of such claim in writing and given authority, and full and proper information and assistance (at Company's expense) for the defense of same.
findings.why: Company's patent/IP indemnification obligation is uncapped — it covers every expense, damage, cost, and loss with no stated monetary ceiling.

findings.quote: Distributor shall indemnify and hold the Company and its affiliates harmless from and against any and all claims, judgments, costs, awards, expenses (including reasonable attorney fees) and liabilities of every kind arising from Distributor's fault or negligence in its use of the Snotarator ™ mark and in the marketing and distribution of the Products within the Territory.
findings.why: Distributor's trademark indemnification obligation covers liabilities of every kind with no cap.

findings.quote: IN NO EVENT SHALL COMPANY BE LIABLE FOR ANY INCIDENTAL OR CONSEQUENTIAL DAMAGES AND DISTRIBUTOR'S REMEDIES SHALL BE LIMITED TO REPAIR OR REPLACEMENT OF NONCONFORMING UNITS OR PARTS.
findings.why: Caps Company's liability under the warranty to repair or replacement only, explicitly excluding incidental and consequential damages.

findings.quote: The term of this Agreement shall terminate on May 31, 2015, unless sooner terminated. Termination shall not relieve either party of obligations incurred prior thereto.  The term of this Agreement may be extended for an additional two year period with the written consent of both parties.
findings.why: Sets a fixed term with an extension mechanism requiring mutual written consent — not automatic, but flags the renewal mechanism.

findings.quote: By the Company if: there is an unacceptable change in the control or management of the Distributor; if the Distributor  makes an assignment for the benefit of creditors; if a petition in bankruptcy is filed by or against the Distributor, resulting in an adjudication of bankruptcy; or, if the Distributor fails to pay its debts as they become due and provided due notice has been given by the Company to the Distributor and the Distributor has not cured such breach within thirty (30) days thereof;
findings.why: Company may terminate upon unacceptable change in control or management of Distributor, triggering termination rights on ownership/control change.

findings.quote: The Distributor agrees to hold the Company free and harmless from any and all claims, damages, and expenses of every kind or nature whatsoever (a) arising from acts of the Distributor; (b) as a direct or indirect consequence of termination of this Agreement in accordance with its terms; or (c) arising from acts of third parties in relation to Products sold to the Distributor under this Agreement, including, but not limited to execution of liens and security interests by third parties with respect to any such Products.
findings.why: Distributor's general indemnification obligation covers claims of every kind with no monetary cap.

findings.quote: This Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the parties and their successors and assigns.  Distributor shall not assign this Agreement or any rights or obligations hereunder without the prior written consent of the Company.
findings.why: Restricts Distributor from assigning the entire Agreement or any rights/obligations without Company's prior written consent.
